news.title:
Dolat Capital sees IT sector valuations corrected; Infosys, LTIMindtree, and mid-tier players like Coforge, Persistent Systems, KPIT excel.
AI-led spending and execution-focused strategies to drive selective growth; mid-tier firms show 17-25% CAGR outperformance.
news.summary:

news.title:
Motilal Oswal recommends TCS, HCLTech, Tech Mahindra, Infosys, & Coforge ahead of Q4 results.
HCLTech, Tech Mahindra, and Coforge noted for resilience, growth, and strong execution by Motilal Oswal.

news.title:
Infosys received tax assessment orders for multiple years, expecting a refund of ₹1,745 crore with interest.
Refund pertains to AYs 2013-14 to 2021-22; financial impact for FY 2025-26 under evaluation.
news.summary:
Infosys received tax assessment orders for multiple years, expecting a refund of ₹1,745 crore with interest.

news.title:
JM Financial prefers Infosys for large-cap IT and Mphasis for mid-tier growth ahead of Q4FY26 results.
Infosys projects 2-4% FY27 revenue growth; NSE IT faces 15% underperformance amid AI and geopolitical concerns.
news.summary:
JM Financial prefers Infosys for large-cap IT and Mphasis for mid-tier growth ahead of Q4FY26 results.

news.title:
Indian mid-sized IT firms like Persistent, Hexaware, and Firstsource focus on Asia and Africa for growth amid US slowdown.
Persistent diversifies via acquisitions; Infosys, Wipro expand in Australia; TCS grows in India with large contracts.
news.summary:
Indian mid-sized IT firms like Persistent, Hexaware, and Firstsource focus on Asia and Africa for growth amid US slowdown.

news.title:
Nifty IT fell 1.5% as fears of Iran's threats to subsea cables heightened risks to India's digital connectivity.
TCS rose, while Infosys, Persistent Systems, OFSS, LTIMindtree, and others declined. Analysts see volatility ahead for IT stocks.
news.summary:
Nifty IT fell 1.5% as fears of Iran's threats to subsea cables heightened risks to India's digital connectivity.
TCS rose, while Infosys, Persistent Systems, OFSS, LTIMindtree, and others declined. Analysts see volatility ahead for IT stocks.

news.title:
Mcap of 7 top firms fell by ₹1.75 trn; Reliance lost ₹89,720.3 cr, HDFC Bank ₹37,248.6 cr.
L&T, Bajaj Finance, Infosys gained; geopolitical tensions drove market volatility, Sensex fell 1.3%.
news.summary:
